title: Quarterly Physical Access List Review Exception Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 12.1.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 12.1.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.60: Quarterly Physical Access List Review Exception Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.60' running on index=grc sourcetype="grc:control_test" — control_id, test_status, due_date, owner, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype="grc:control_test" earliest=-120d
| where control_id IN ("PCI-9.2.4-PHYS-REVIEW","PCI-9.2.4")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_date AND NOT match(test_status,"(?i)pass|complete"),1,0)
| stats latest(test_status) as status, latest(due_date) as due by owner
| where overdue=1 OR isnull(status)
| sort due
description: Tracks completion of quarterly access roster reviews for personnel with keys or badges to CDE facilities, tying physical security policy to measurable workflow deadlines.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes grc:control_test. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Create recurring GRC control with quarterly `due_date`. (2) Push completion events from ServiceNow attestation. (3) Escalate to facility security lead. (4) Export closed tests for PCI policy evidence (Req 12 linkage). Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=grc:control_test`.
visualization: Table (overdue owners), timeline (due_date), single value (overdue count).
knownFalsePositives: Firewall changes during scheduled change windows, controlled segmentation rollouts, or planned audits.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), `sourcetype="grc:control_test"`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:control_test"` — `control_id`, `test_status`, `due_date`, `owner`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Create recurring GRC control with quarterly `due_date`. (2) Push completion events from ServiceNow attestation. (3) Escalate to facility security lead. (4) Export closed tests for PCI policy evidence (Req 12 linkage).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:control_test" earliest=-120d
| where control_id IN ("PCI-9.2.4-PHYS-REVIEW","PCI-9.2.4")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_date AND NOT match(test_status,"(?i)pass|complete"),1,0)
| stats latest(test_status) as status, latest(due_date) as due by owner
| where overdue=1 OR isnull(status)
| sort due
```

Understanding this SPL

**Quarterly Physical Access List Review Exception Tracking (PCI DSS Req 9.2.4, 12.1.1)** — Tracks completion of quarterly access roster reviews for personnel with keys or badges to CDE facilities, tying physical security policy to measurable workflow deadlines.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:control_test"` — `control_id`, `test_status`, `due_date`, `owner`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), `sourcetype="grc:control_test"`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: grc:control_test. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="grc:control_test", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where control_id IN ("PCI-9.2.4-PHYS-REVIEW","PCI-9.2.4")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overdue** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by owner** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where overdue=1 OR isnull(status)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue owners), timeline (due_date), single value (overdue count).
grandmaExplanation: We track completion of quarterly access roster reviews for personnel with keys or badges to CDE facilities, tying physical security policy to measurable workflow deadlines. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model), All_Changes dataset.
